2011-07-27.abstract: George Osborne, the chancellor of the Exchequer, had extensive meetings with Rupert and James Murdoch.

2011-07-27.title: James Murdoch’s Future at Stake as BSkyB Board Meets
2011-07-27.abstract: On Thursday, BSkyB’s board will meet for the first time since the phone hacking outcry forced News Corporation to drop its bid to buy the network.

2011-07-27.title: Security Council Debate Offers Preview of Palestinian Bid
2011-07-27.abstract: A preview of the expected showdown over whether to admit a Palestinian state as a full member of the United Nations in September played out in the Security Council on Tuesday.

2011-07-27.title: Killer, 15, Is Sentenced in Drug Case in Mexico
2011-07-27.abstract: Edgar Jiménez Lugo, hired by a Mexican drug cartel, was convicted of beheading four men and sentenced to three years in prison.

2011-07-28.title: Battle for Libya | July 27, 2011
2011-07-28.abstract: British Foreign Secretary William Hague announced formal diplomatic recognition of the Libyan rebels on Wednesday.

2011-07-28.title: Before a Diplomatic Showdown, a Budget Crisis Saps Palestinians’ Confidence
2011-07-28.abstract: Economic woes are mixed with a diplomatic crisis as Palestinians start a statehood push.

2011-07-28.title: U.S. Loses Ally as Iraqi General Waits for Trial
2011-07-28.abstract: The fate of Gen. Nomon Dakhil is a uniquely Iraqi tale of intrigue involving charges of corruption and competing versions of reality, showing the tension the United States and an increasingly assertive Iraqi government.

2011-07-28.title: Eulogies for Bin Laden, Shrouded in Mystery
2011-07-28.abstract: Who are the secret sponsors of a poetry and essay contest eulogizing Osama bin Laden at Pakistan’s largest university?

2011-07-28.title: Norwegians Concede a Role in Chilean Salmon Virus
2011-07-28.abstract: A Norwegian aquaculture company endorsed a scientific study that found salmon eggs shipped from Norway to Chile are the “likely reason” for a devastating virus outbreak in 2007.

2011-07-29.title: In Baring Facts of Train Crash, Blogs Erode China Censorship
2011-07-29.abstract: China’s major Twitter-like microblogs posted an astounding 26 million messages on the crash, a potent amalgam of contempt, suspicion and shoe-leather journalism.

2011-07-29.title: Ex-Counterterrorism Aide Warns Against Complacency on Al Qaeda
2011-07-29.abstract: Michael E. Leiter, former head of the National Counterterrorism Center, dismissed the idea that Al Qaeda was near collapse.

2011-07-29.title: Battle for Libya | July 28, 2011
2011-07-29.abstract: Supporters of Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi of Libya appeared for a rally in Tripoli on Thursday evening.

2011-07-29.title: In Norway, Consensus Cuts 2 Ways
2011-07-29.abstract: Norway’s consensus culture or Jante Law, may have taken a blow after the killings last Friday in Oslo.

2011-07-29.title: Japanese, in Shortage, Willingly Ration Watts
2011-07-29.abstract: Since the nuclear disaster, Japan has pushed to conserve electricity, even as it tries to revive its economy.

2011-07-30.title: On the Party Circuit, and Upsetting the Party
2011-07-30.abstract: Chan Koonchung’s novel predicts an Orwellian future in China while examining life in an authoritarian state.

2011-07-30.title: Sound of Gunfire From Island Sent Civilians to Teenagers’ Rescue
2011-07-30.abstract: Norwegian authorities have credited civilian rescuers with keeping the death toll of the massacre on Utoya island from rising even higher.

2011-07-30.title: In Uganda, a Failure to Deliver
2011-07-30.abstract: A lawsuit over the death of a woman in labor may be the first legal test of an African government’s obligation to provide basic maternal care and also raises questions about the impact of foreign aid.

2011-07-30.title: Maternal Deaths Focus Harsh Light on Uganda
2011-07-30.abstract: Uganda is putting less money toward health care, leaving hospitals unprepared to safely deliver babies.

2011-07-30.title: In Belarus, Just Being Can Prompt an Arrest
2011-07-30.abstract: Cracking down on increasingly subtle silent protests, Belarussian authorities prohibit standing together and doing nothing.

2011-07-31.title: In Afghanistan, Rage at Young Lovers
2011-07-31.abstract: A romance between two teenagers that flouted traditions of arranged marriages aroused anger and violence.

2011-07-31.title: Defenses Are Thickened at Afghan Border to Choke Off Militants’ Routes
2011-07-31.abstract: American and allied forces in Afghanistan are strengthening a layered defense along the border with Pakistan to seize Haqqani network militants.

2011-07-31.title: Syrians Strike Restive Cities in Fierce Raids
2011-07-31.abstract: Scenes of bloodshed in several cities around Syria reveal the government’s new resolve to crack down on dissent.

2011-07-31.title: Media Blackout in China After Wreck
2011-07-31.abstract: Chinese authorities enacted a virtual news blackout on last month’s high-speed train crash amid public fury.

2011-07-31.title: Deadly Violence Strikes Chinese City Racked by Ethnic Tensions
2011-07-31.abstract: Eighteen people were killed in the ancient city of Kashgar, in the far western Xinjiang region.
